revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:第六天神社 (茅ヶ崎市)]]
[[File:Dairokuten jinjya (Chigasaki, Kanagawa) 05.JPG|thumb|Dairokuten Shrine (Chigasaki City, Kanagawa)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Dairokuten Shrine |image = File:Dairokuten jinjya (Chigasaki, Kanagawa) 05.JPG |location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}[[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki]] Jukkenzaka 3-17-18 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.33021388888889|N|139.39667777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Omodaru and Ayakashikone|qid=Q11152535|1=Omodaru and Ayakashikone}} |Rank = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Former Village shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office Designated Shrine|ja|神奈川県神社庁指定神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office Designated Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神奈川県の神社一覧|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神奈川県の神社一覧|1=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office Designated Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11588941}} |established = Unknown |other_names = |main_festival = [[April 29]] |rituals = |location = {{ill|Chigasaki|qid=Q633911|1=Chigasaki}} }} '''Dairokuten Shrine''' (第六天神社, ''Dairokuten Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki, Kanagawa]] at Jukkensaka. During the period of {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|qid=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}, it was also called '''Dairokuten-sha'''<ref name="tabino-michishirube-dairokuten">[http://aralagi.travel-way.net/jinja/dairokuten.html Dairokuten Shrine] (Tabi no Michishirube)</ref>. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Omodaru and Ayakashikone|ja|淤母陀琉神|lt=Omodaru-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omodaru and Ayakashikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オモダル・アヤカシコネ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オモダル・アヤカシコネ}}<ref name="syatou-goyuisyo">Shrine bulletin board "Dairokuten Shrine History"</ref> * {{ill|Omodaru and Ayakashikone|ja|阿夜訶志古泥神|lt=Ayakashikone-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omodaru and Ayakashikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オモダル・アヤカシコネ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オモダル・アヤカシコネ}}<ref name="syatou-goyuisyo"/> :※ After the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|qid=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} of the Meiji Restoration, the deity was changed from the original deity {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|第六天魔王|lt=Dairokuten Maō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天魔|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天魔}} (Takejizaiten). (See [[#History|section below]] for details.) ; Divine Virtues In recent times, the shrine is revered as a protective deity for production, manufacturing, and construction. Originally, the deity Dairokuten Maō, whose stature was said to be about 2 {{ill|Li (unit)|en|Li (unit)|qid=Q654875|lt=Ri (unit)|1=Li (unit)}} (approximately 8 km), had a lifespan of 16,000 years, each day being 1,600 human years. He was worshipped for physical strength, longevity, and safety for travelers along the {{ill|Tōkaidō (region)|en|Tōkaidō (region)|qid=Q1196306|lt=Old Tōkaidō|1=Tōkaidō (region)}}<ref name="kanagawajinja-guidebook">''Kanagawa Shrine Guidebook'' (Kanashin Publishing, 1997), p83</ref>. == History == ; Foundation Period The shrine's founding date is unknown, but it first appeared in the historical record "{{ill|Shinpen Sagami no Kuni Fudoki-kō|ja|新編相模国風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Sagami no Kuni Fudoki-kō|1=Shinpen Sagami no Kuni Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11503942}}" compiled by the {{ill|Edo shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1841 (Tenpō 12)<ref name="kanagawajinja-guidebook"/>. While it is traditionally said to have been founded in the early Edo period, records from the ''{{ill|Azuma Kagami|en|Azuma Kagami|qid=Q1042324|lt=Azuma Kagami|1=Azuma Kagami}}'' indicate that the place-name "Jukkensaka" dates back to at least 1332 (Genkō 3), when {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|en|Nitta Yoshisada|qid=Q1328796|lt=Nitta Yoshisada|1=Nitta Yoshisada}} attacked Kamakura, suggesting a possible foundation during the Kamakura period<ref name="tabino-michishirube-dairokuten"/><ref name="kanagawa-jinjyacho-chigasaki-dairokuten"/>. ; Shinbutsu Bunri and Deity Change Until the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|qid=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} of 1872, Dairokuten Shrine was protected by {{ill|Kongōin|ja|金剛院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongōin|1=Kongō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11646549}}, located in Chigasaki's Nango district. Post-separation, it became the local tutelary shrine for Jukkensaka, Chigasaki City, and its deity was changed from {{ill|Dairokuten Maō|ja|第六天魔王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Maō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天魔|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天魔|1=Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q13578873}} (Takejizaiten) to its current gods, Omodaru and Ayakashikone<ref name="kanagawajinja-guidebook"/>. == Shrine Grounds == On the shrine grounds, various sub-shrines and stone Buddhas are enshrined. Behind the worship hall is a sacred {{ill|Pinus thunbergii|en|Pinus thunbergii|qid=Q854884|lt=Kuromatsu|1=Pinus thunbergii}}, with a diameter of approximately 95 cm<ref name="chigasaki-no-sekibutsu14">''Chigasaki Stone Buddhas'', No.14 (2010)</ref>. === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|qid=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=Yasaka Shrine}} - Deity: {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. Originally on the west side, its deity was relocated during reconstruction in 1992. === Stone Buddhas === To the right of the haiden are statues like {{ill|Roku Jizō|ja|六地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Roku Jizō|1=Six Jizō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11392304}}, Kōshin-tō, and Dōsojin. == Festivals and Annual Events == ; Major Festivals * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} * January 14: {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|en|Sagichō Fire Festival|qid=Q9332740|lt=Sagichō|1=Sagichō Fire Festival}} (Don-don-yaki) * February 17: {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|qid=Q118906710|lt=Kinen-sai|1=Kinensai}} * April 29: Main Festival * June 30: {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|en|Ōharae-shiki|qid=Q11261910|lt=Ōharae|1=Ōharae-shiki}} * Marine Day in July: {{ill|Hamaori-sai|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori-sai|1=Hamaori Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11558084}} * September: Memorial for War Dead ; Annual Event * December 27: Daruma Market<ref>[http://www.shonan-sh.jp/columns/1382 Toko-toko Shonan]</ref> == Shrine Treasures == In 1992, the current Honden and Heiden were rebuilt. The original main hall is now Yasaka Shrine’s hall. == Location & Access == 3-17-18 Jukkensaka, Chigasaki, Kanagawa Prefecture Accessible via JR Chigasaki Station. == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=668 Kanagawa Shrine Office]
<references />
{{Dairokuten Faith|qq=}}
